title: The Task Force Found Something
trigger: Your anti-corruption task force was supposed to be theater. It wasn't supposed to actually find anything.
description: Three weeks into the ghost-payroll investigation, the task force lead requests an urgent private meeting. The trail of fake employees doesn't end at the sanitation department — it runs through the {city.government_body}'s procurement office and stops one door from yours. The lead investigator is honest, ambitious, and currently deciding what to do with the file.

options.label: Let the investigation run its course, wherever it leads.
options.advisor_reason: Let it run. We bleed a little now, or we hemorrhage when someone leaks that we stopped it. Cheap insurance, all things considered.

consequences.follow_up: Two procurement officials resign. The press calls it 'unexpectedly genuine'. The deputy mayor stops returning your calls.
consequences.city_reaction: An investigation that investigated. In THIS city. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Promote the lead investigator — to a desk in another department.
options.advisor_reason: Promote the problem. A bigger title, a quieter office, and the file inherits a successor who understands nuance. Costs us a salary bump, saves us a headline.

consequences.follow_up: The investigator accepts the promotion with a smile that doesn't reach their eyes. The file is 'transitioning between custodians'.
consequences.city_reaction: Promoted for finding corruption. Sure. And I'm the new finance minister. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Leak the file yourself — all of it, names included, tonight.
options.advisor_reason: Burn it all down before someone burns it down on top of us. If every name drops at once, yours is just one in the pile — and you're the one who lit the match in public.

consequences.follow_up: The city wakes up to forty names and one question: who leaked it? Half the {city.government_body} isn't speaking to the other half. Or to you.
consequences.city_reaction: The governor leaked his own government. This place is a reality show. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Shattered Streets of {city.name}
trigger: The rapid cooling shattered the asphalt completely and tragically froze three stray dogs solid.
description: Your 300M liquid nitrogen climate initiative was a catastrophic success. The localized temperature plummeted, but the extreme thermal shock turned downtown {city.name}'s infrastructure into jagged, impassable gravel. Emergency vehicles are stranded, the {city.primary_industry_workers} are unable to commute, and furious animal rights activists are holding a deeply unsettling candlelight vigil around the flash-frozen strays currently thawing near {city.landmark}. The {city.media_outlet} is running live aerial footage of the ruined grid.

options.label: Deploy emergency crews to repave the major arteries with premium cold-weather asphalt.
options.advisor_reason: It costs us another 140M, but we have to get ambulances moving again. We eat the horrific cost, pretend it was aggressively scheduled maintenance all along, and desperately try to buy back the public's forgiveness.

consequences.follow_up: The roads are finally fixed, but the treasury is hollowed out and municipal bond ratings plummet.
consequences.city_reaction: They broke the entire city just to overcharge us to fix it. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Ban all vehicles downtown and declare the jagged rubble a 'historical walking path'.
options.advisor_reason: If we explicitly ban cars, the broken roads suddenly become brilliant pedestrian traffic calming measures. It costs barely 20M to print new signage and spins an infrastructure disaster as an aggressive green urban pedestrianization project.

consequences.follow_up: Commuters are forced to hike through sharp asphalt chunks. Emergency response times triple, but carbon emissions drop.
consequences.city_reaction: My ankles are bleeding profusely, but at least there is absolutely no traffic. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Sweep the shattered asphalt into massive mounds and sell them as avant-garde public art.
options.advisor_reason: The roads are already destroyed. Let us deploy bulldozers, sweep the rubble into massive piles, shine some spotlights on them, and charge tourists 50M to witness our brilliant 'commentary on urban decay and climate fragility'.

consequences.follow_up: An international art critic calls the asphalt piles 'transcendent'. An elderly citizen falls off one and sues the city for millions.
consequences.city_reaction: We are living in a literal garbage dump and they call it high art. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Spa Day at the Hague
trigger: Rebranding the toxic river as a spa resulted in tourists getting severe chemical burns and international lawsuits.
description: The 'Hot Springs Wellness Initiative' has triggered a public health and legal apocalypse. International health tribunals are threatening to freeze the {city.government_body}'s assets after the bubbling chemical sludge dissolved the epidermal layers of visiting dignitaries. To make matters worse, the mayor's legally blind cousin—who approved the environmental inspection—is publicly demanding hazard pay for operating the spa's ticket booth. The {city.media_outlet} is currently running a 24/7 telethon to pay for mass skin grafts.

options.label: Settle the international lawsuits quietly and pay for the tourists' chemical burns.
options.advisor_reason: We must pay 150M to settle this instantly before the international health organizations completely bankrupt our entire municipality. We made a massive mistake letting the mayor's cousin anywhere near this.

consequences.follow_up: The tourists receive extensive skin grafts and sign ironclad NDAs. The toxic river continues to bubble quietly in the background.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad to see my local taxes paying for an international billionaire's new epidermis. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Counter-sue the burned tourists for stealing proprietary municipal chemical compounds.
options.advisor_reason: They willingly bathed in it. For 30M in aggressive legal fees, we can argue in court that the chemical burns are actually unauthorized, parasitic absorption of valuable city property. Offense is the best defense.

consequences.follow_up: The international community places severe economic sanctions on the city. The sheer audacity of the counter-suit makes global legal history.
consequences.city_reaction: We poisoned them and then sued them for bleeding. Utterly savage. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Market the skin-peeling water as an exclusive, radical anti-aging exfoliation therapy.
options.advisor_reason: If we spend 50M on ultra-luxury marketing campaigns, we can convince paranoid billionaires that third-degree chemical burns are just aggressive cellular renewal. We can turn a massive profit from this horror.

consequences.follow_up: Wealthy international influencers start visiting the toxic sludge intentionally. The United Nations issues a severe human rights warning against the city.
consequences.city_reaction: Rich people are literally paying our government to melt their faces off. Beautiful. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Auditing the Rain Gods
trigger: The shaman danced for three days and failed to produce rain, prompting a massive state audit for fraudulent expenses.
description: State auditors have arrived at the {city.government_body} with briefcases full of subpoenas, demanding itemized, logical receipts for the shaman's 150M 'cloud-coaxing and atmospheric manipulation fees'. The spectacle failed to produce a single drop of rain. Furthermore, the shaman used the massive consulting fee to purchase a fleet of luxury water trucks, and is currently driving through the drought-stricken districts selling the city's own stolen reserve water back to the dehydrated {city.primary_industry_workers} at a terrifying premium markup.

options.label: Comply fully with the state audit and publicly write off the shaman's fee as a total loss.
options.advisor_reason: We eat a 120M investigative penalty and cooperate fully. The shaman danced for three days and we got absolutely nothing. If we attempt to hide the magical fraud now, the state will remove us from office in handcuffs.

consequences.follow_up: The auditors fine the city heavily and seize administrative controls. The shaman disappears into the desert with the original money.
consequences.city_reaction: We paid millions for a magic trick and got federally audited. Brilliant leadership. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Confiscate the shaman's luxury water trucks using the municipal police force.
options.advisor_reason: The shaman used our taxpayer money to buy water trucks. Let's use 40M to militarize our police, seize the assets by force, and distribute the reclaimed water to the dying golf courses and furious citizens.

consequences.follow_up: A violent, publicized shootout occurs between the city police and the shaman's private security. The water is successfully reclaimed.
consequences.city_reaction: City hall acting like apocalyptic warlords over a few gallons of water. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Bribe the auditors with remaining municipal funds to participate in a 'spiritual cleansing' retreat.
options.advisor_reason: If we spend another 150M to send the auditors to the shaman's new luxury spiritual retreat, they might achieve enlightenment and lose the paperwork. We must double down on the magic to survive.

consequences.follow_up: The auditors return wearing flowing robes and refuse to speak about the city's finances. The federal government prepares to intervene.
consequences.city_reaction: They literally bought off the state feds with a cult retreat. We live in a dystopia. {city.slang_reaction}.
